step.title: Scaffold Ticket Bundle
step.goal: Invoke the scaffold-bundle.py script with the confirmed slug, topic, and primary tag. The script creates the bundle directory and three schema-valid markdown files (ticket, research, questions). Verify the output before proceeding to handoff.

substep.title: Invoke scaffold-bundle.py
substep.note: The script: 1. Calls `thoughts metadata` internally (re-fetching, NOT relying on our session.* values — this is the script's contract and ensures a fresh timestamp on the bundle files). 2. Creates {{session.shared_folder}}/tickets/{{session.slug}}/ if it does not exist; errors out if the directory already exists. 3. Writes three files: - ticket-YYYY-MM-DD-{{session.slug}}.md (kind: ticket, status: draft) - research-YYYY-MM-DD-{{session.slug}}.md (kind: research, status: active) - questions-YYYY-MM-DD-{{session.slug}}.md (kind: questions, status: active) - worklog-YYYY-MM-DD-{{session.slug}}.md (kind: worklog, status: active) 4. Populates frontmatter with both `owner:` and `researcher:`, full ISO 8601 `date:` and `last_updated:`, schema-valid `parent:` (on sibling files) and `children:` (on the ticket), tags array, and the canonical alias as first element of `aliases:`. 5. Returns the three created paths on stdout for capture.

substep.title: Verify Outputs
Display Scaffold Result
substep.template-output:
Bundle directory created: {{session.bundle_dir}}/
Files created: - {{session.ticket_path}} (kind: ticket, status: draft) - {{session.research_path}} (kind: research, status: active) - {{session.questions_path}} (kind: questions, status: active) - {{session.worklog_path}} (kind: worklog, status: active)
All three pass `thoughts validate`. Schema-native frontmatter populated.

section.title: The script's encapsulation contract
section.description: scaffold-bundle.py invokes `thoughts metadata` internally. This is deliberate per Lock 27: the script is self-contained, and the agent passes only domain arguments (slug, topic, primary-tag). Do NOT pass owner, date, or repo context as arguments — the script fetches those itself to guarantee freshness.

section.title: If the bundle directory already exists

rules.rule: The script errors out by design. The agent should NOT pass --force or any override
Surface the conflict to the user via Question tool: "A bundle with slug X already exists. Use a different slug, or are you resuming a previous session?"
If resuming: the user should invoke ticket-grill directly with the existing bundle, not re-run ticket-orient
If different work: ask for a new slug and loop back to step 5 (scope checkpoint) to reconfirm
If `thoughts validate` reports errors, halt the workflow
Surface the raw validation output to the user
Do NOT auto-fix frontmatter — that is the script's job; if the script produced invalid output, the script needs fixing, not the file
Report as a bug in scaffold-bundle.py and abort the session

section.title: Frontmatter sanity check
section.description: Per Lock 32, the script emits BOTH `owner:` AND `researcher:` fields populated from the same `thoughts metadata` owner value. It emits full ISO 8601 datetime for `last_updated:` (not date-only — the validator requires full timestamps). It emits `[[alias]]` wiki-link form for `parent:` and `children:`. If any of these are missing or malformed, validation will fail in substep 6b.
